chunks.text: Stereo. HC JD A 38. Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, BAHAWALPUR BENCH, BAHAWALPUR. JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 (The State Vs. Azhar) Criminal Appeal No. 267 of 2017 (Azhar Vs. The State and another.) Date of hearing: 21.09.2021 Appellant by: Syed Zeeshan Haider, Advocate. State by: Complainant by: Ch. Asghar Ali Gill, Deputy Prosecutor General. Jam Fakhar-ud-Din Lar,Advocate. JUDGMENT. SADIQ MAHMUD KHURRAM, J.– Azhar son of Hashim (convict) was tried by the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Sadiqabad alongwith Akbar son of Jeevan (since acquitted) in the case FIR No. 19 of 2015 dated 24.01.2015 registered at Police Station Ahmadpur Lamma, District Rahim Yar Khan in respect of offences under sections 302 and 34 PPC for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Mst. Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Habib Ahmad (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 28.04.2017, convicted Azhar son of Hashim (convict) and sentenced him as under: Azhar son of Hashim: Death under section 302(b) PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Mst. Mst. Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 2 Habib Ahmad (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.1,00,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of Mst. Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Habib Ahmad (deceased) and in case of default thereof, the convict was further directed to undergo six months of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. Akbar son of Jeewan, the co-accused of the appellant, was however acquitted by the learned trial court. 2. Feeling aggrieved, Azhar son of Hashim (convict) lodged Criminal Appeal No.267 of 2017 assailing his conviction and sentence.

Akbar son of Jeewan, the co-accused of the appellant, was however acquitted by the learned trial court. 2. Feeling aggrieved, Azhar son of Hashim (convict) lodged Criminal Appeal No.267 of 2017 assailing his conviction and sentence. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.22 of 2017 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentence of death awarded to the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim. We intend to dispose of the Criminal Appeal No. 267 of 2017 and Murder Reference No.22 of 2017 through this single judgment. 3. Precisely, the necessary facts of the prosecution case, as narrated by Imam Bakhsh (PW-1), the complainant of the case, are as under:- “About five/six years ago, Mst. Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Habib Ahmad, caste Detha, r/o Mauza Qasmani, married with me with her own free will and consent. Before our marriage, Azhar son of Hashim accused present in court was engaged with Mst. Allah Rakhi. Azhar was became revenge hulk why Allah Rakhi married with me. On 24-01-2015, at about 10:00 am, I alongwith my wife Allah Rakhi, Ghulam Haider and Haji Ahmad went to sugar cane crop for cutting the crop. At about 01:30 pm, after cutting the crop, I alongwith Allah Rakhi my wife, Ghulam Haider my brother and Haji Ahmad were coming back from our field towards our house having with fodder. Allah Rakhi was at a distance from us at some Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 3 paces and we were behind Allah Rakhi alongwith Haji Ahmad and Ghulam Haider at some distance. When we reached at the brink Kandair raw road, and then Akbar and Azhar armed with pistol came at the spot. Upon seeing us, Akbar raised lalkara while armed with pistol that we will teach the lesson to Allah Rakhi upon getting marriage with Imam Bakhsh and we will not leave her alive. Then, in the meanwhile, Azhar accused made fire shot in order to kill Allah Rakhi straight away made fire shot upon Allah Rakhi which hit on the right palm of hand of Allah Rakhi deceased.

Upon seeing us, Akbar raised lalkara while armed with pistol that we will teach the lesson to Allah Rakhi upon getting marriage with Imam Bakhsh and we will not leave her alive. Then, in the meanwhile, Azhar accused made fire shot in order to kill Allah Rakhi straight away made fire shot upon Allah Rakhi which hit on the right palm of hand of Allah Rakhi deceased. When, I alongwith Haji Ahmad and Ghulam Haider went forward just in order to save Allah Rakhi then Akbar accused frightened us by way of making slogans that if anybody come near, he will kill us, Azhar made second fire shot to Allah Rakhi deceased which hit on her chest. Allah Rakhi after receiving fire shot injury fell down on the earth. Then Azhar made third fire shot to Allah Rakhi which hit on her back. I Ghulam Haider and Haji Ahmad raised hue and cry after this event. People gathered there after hearing the voices of this occurrence and the above said accused decamped from the spot. I alongwith Ghulam Haider, Haji Ahmad tried to manage Mst. Allah Rakhi but she could not succumbed the injuries she died on the spot. The above-said accused are killer of my wife, they must be punished severely in accordance with law. 1.0 came at the spot and got recorded my statement Exh. PA which bears my signatures Exh.PA/1. The same statement was read over by the I.O to me on the spot.” 4. After the formal investigation of the case report under section 173 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 was submitted before the learned trial court wherein the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim and the co-accused of the appellant namely Akbar son of Jeewan (since Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 4 acquitted) were sent to face trial. The learned trial court framed the charge against the accused on 23.02.2016, to which the accused pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. 5. The prosecution in order to prove its case got statements of as many as ten witnesses recorded. The ocular account of the case was furnished by Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2).

The prosecution in order to prove its case got statements of as many as ten witnesses recorded. The ocular account of the case was furnished by Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2). Altaf Hussain, SI (PW-3) stated that on 24.01.2015 he recorded the formal F.I.R (Exh.PF).Ali Bakhsh 1492/HC (PW-5) stated that on 23.03.2015 he handed over two sealed parcels said to contain the recovered empties and pistol to Muhammad Ramzan 669/C (PW-8) for their onward transmission to the office of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. Shahid Mehmood 1069/C (PW-6) stated that on 24.01.2015 he escorted the dead body of the deceased for its post mortem examination and also received the last worn clothes of the deceased from the Medical Officer. Mudassar Maqsood 394/HC (PW-7) stated that on 24.01.2015 the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to me two sealed parcels and on 23.03.2015 he handed over both the said parcels to Muhammad Ramzan 669/C (PW-8) for their onward transmission to the office of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. Liaqat Ali Patwari (PW-9) prepared the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PH).Jam Munir Ahmad Inspector (PW-10), the Investigating Officer of the case, investigated the case from 24.01.2015 till 12.08.2016, arrested the appellant on 19.02.2015, and detailed the facts of the investigation as conducted by him in his statement before the learned trial court. Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 5 6. The prosecution also got Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4) examined, who on 24.01.2015 was posted as Woman Medical Officer at RHC, Ahmadpur Lamma and on the same day conducted the postmortem examination of the dead body of Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Habib Ahmad (deceased). Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4),on examining the dead body of Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Habib Ahmad (deceased) observed as under:- “Injury No.1 Multiple circular and irregular lacerated wound with inverted margins was present in front of chest in the central region each measuring 0.7x 0.7 cm about 7 cm from left nipple and 5 cm from right nipple and about 6 cm from manubrium sterni.

Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4),on examining the dead body of Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Habib Ahmad (deceased) observed as under:- “Injury No.1 Multiple circular and irregular lacerated wound with inverted margins was present in front of chest in the central region each measuring 0.7x 0.7 cm about 7 cm from left nipple and 5 cm from right nipple and about 6 cm from manubrium sterni. Blackening was present around the wound. Bleeding also present. Injury No.2 9 cm x 9 cm avulsed wound was present on lateral aspect of palmer surface of right hand. Wound was irregular and flush(sic) exposed. It was about 23 cm from right elbow joint. Injury No.3 (a) A lacerated wound with irregular everted margin measuring 03 cm x 03 cm was present on the back on left side. It was about 20 cm away from anterior superior illac supine of left side and 12 cm away from posterior axilliary line of left side. (b) Irregular lacerated wound with everted margin measuring 3 cm x 4 cm was present on back on right side about 24 cm from anterior superior iliac supine of right side and 15 cm away from posterior axilliary line of right side. Injury No.1 and Injury No.3 correlates with each other........................................ Opinion: In my opinion, the cause of death was hemorrhagic shock due to injury No. 1 and 3. Injury No. 1,2 and 3 were individually and collectively sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature. Injury No.1 is entry wound and injury No.3 was exit wound. All injuries was caused by fire-arm and was antimortem in nature. Probable Time that elapsed: Between injury and death immediately as per police papers. Between death and postmortem: Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 6 Within four hours.. Final Opinion: In my opinion, the cause of death was hemorrhagic shock due to injury No.1 and 3. Injury No. 1,2 and 3 were individually and collectively sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature. Injury No.1 is entry wound and injury No.3 was exit wound. All injuries was caused by fire-arm and was antimortem in nature.” 7.

Injury No.1 is entry wound and injury No.3 was exit wound. All injuries was caused by fire-arm and was antimortem in nature.” 7. On 15.12.2016 the learned ADPP gave up the prosecution witness namely Haji Ahmad as being unnecessary and closed the prosecution evidence after tendering in evidence the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PM) regarding the analysis of the blood stained earth and the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PN) regarding the analysis of the crime empties and the recovered weapon. 8. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to the question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim replied that he had been involved in the case falsely and was innocent. The appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim opted not to get himself examined under section 340 Cr.P.C. and however, got Muhammad Khalid Raza (DW-1), Muhammad Zia-ud-Din (DW-2), Muhammad Hashim (DW-3) and Rana Khan (DW-4) examined as witnesses in his defence. 9. On the conclusion of the trial, the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Sadiqabad, convicted and sentenced the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim as referred to above. Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 7 10. The contention of the learned counsel for the appellant precisely is that the whole case is fabricated and false and the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible, and relevant evidence. Learned counsel for the appellant further contended that the story of the prosecution mentioned in the statements of the witnesses, on the face of it, is highly improbable.

The contention of the learned counsel for the appellant precisely is that the whole case is fabricated and false and the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible, and relevant evidence. Learned counsel for the appellant further contended that the story of the prosecution mentioned in the statements of the witnesses, on the face of it, is highly improbable. Learned counsel for the appellant further contended that the statements of the prosecution witnesses were not worthy of any reliance. The learned counsel for the appellant also argued that the appellant had been involved in the occurrence due to enmity. The learned counsel for the appellant finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the accused beyond the shadow of a doubt. 11. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant/respondent No.2 contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond shadow of doubt by producing independent witnesses. They further argued that the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellant. They further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2). They further argued that the recovery of the pistol (P-3) from the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim also corroborated the ocular account. They further contended that there was no occasion for the prosecution witnesses, who were related to the deceased, to substitute the real offenders with the innocent in this case. Lastly, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General and the learned counsel appearing on Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 8 behalf of the complainant/respondent No.2 prayed for the rejection of appeal as lodged by the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim. 12. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellant, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant and with their assistance perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial. 13.

Appeal No. 267 of 2017 8 behalf of the complainant/respondent No.2 prayed for the rejection of appeal as lodged by the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim. 12. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellant, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant and with their assistance perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial. 13. We have noted with grave anxiety that the ocular account of the occurrence as furnished by the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW- 1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) is flawed beyond reparation and has disfigured the appearance of the whole prosecution case beyond restoration and recognition. The statements of both the eye witnesses namely Muhammad Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) are in direct contrast and inconsistent with the observations and the opinion of Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4), who conducted the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased namely Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Habib Ahmad. According to the statements of both the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2),they had seen the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim make three separate fires with the pistol carried by him and all the three fires hit Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Habib Ahmad. Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) in his examination in-chief stated as under:- “Azhar made second fire shot to Allah Rakhi deceased which hit on her chest. Allah Rakhi after receiving fire shot injury fell down on the earth. Then Azhar made third fire shot to Allah Rakhi which hit on her back.” (emphasis supplied). Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 9 Similarly, Ghulam Haider (PW-2) in his examination in-chief stated as under:- “ Then Azhar made second fire shot which landed on chest of Allah Rakhi. Then Azhar accused made third fire shot which landed on the back of Allah Rakhi” (emphasis supplied). Both the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) remained adamant that the appellant had fired thrice at the person of Allah Rakhi (deceased) and further stated that the third fire made by the appellant had hit Allah Rakhi (deceased) on her back.

Then Azhar accused made third fire shot which landed on the back of Allah Rakhi” (emphasis supplied). Both the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) remained adamant that the appellant had fired thrice at the person of Allah Rakhi (deceased) and further stated that the third fire made by the appellant had hit Allah Rakhi (deceased) on her back. Contrary to the said repeated claim of both the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2), Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4), who on 24.01.2015 had conducted the postmortem examination of the dead body of Allah Rakhi daughter of Haji Habib Ahmad (deceased), stated that the injuries observed by her on the back of the body of Allah Rakhi deceased were actually exit wounds relating to the injury No.1 observed by her on the chest of the deceased. Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4) while preparing the post mortem examination (Exh.PF) conclusively opined as under:- “ Injury No.3 (a) A lacerated wound with irregular everted margin measuring 03 cm x 03 cm was present on the back on left side. It was about 20 cm away from anterior superior illac supine of left side and 12 cm away from posterior axilliary line of left side. (b) Irregular lacerated wound with everted margin measuring 3 cm x 4 cm was present on back on right side about 24 cm from anterior superior iliac supine of right side and 15 cm away from posterior axilliary line of right side. Injury No.1 and Injury No.3 correlates with each other........................................ Opinion: In my opinion, the cause of death was hemorrhagic shock due to injury No. 1 and 3. Injury No. 1,2 and 3 were individually and Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 10 collectively sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature. Injury No.1 is entry wound and injury No.3 was exit wound. All injuries was caused by fire-arm and was antimortem in nature................... Final Opinion:.......... Injury No.1 is entry wound and injury No.3 was exit wound.” The said opinion of Dr.

Final Opinion:.......... Injury No.1 is entry wound and injury No.3 was exit wound.” The said opinion of Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4) that the injuries observed by her on the back of the body of Allah Rakhi deceased were actually exit wounds was never challenged by the prosecution either during the investigation of the case or during the course of trial. The prosecution very well knew that the observations and opinion of Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4) were in themselves sufficient to bring down the whole edifice of the prosecution case as narrated by the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2), however, even being in knowledge of this fact, no effort was made by the prosecution witnesses to challenge the observations and opinion of Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4) or if the same could not be challenged, then to explain the same. We have noted that during the course of trial the prosecution witnesses failed to explain as to why was there such a huge, gaping and all consuming, inexplicable and baffling error in the statements of the prosecution witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) who proceeded to attribute causing of a third fire by the appellant on the body of the deceased without any support from the medical evidence. Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4) during cross-examination further explained as under:- It is correct that injury No 1 is entry wound and injury No.3 is exit wound of injury No.1. It is correct that in pictorial diagram I Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 11 mentioned upon injury No.3 which are two parts A and B both are exit wounds. Since injury No.2 which was serious in nature and skin was dragged from the body due to injury that cannot specifically said that which exit wounds upon injury No.2 are.

Appeal No. 267 of 2017 11 mentioned upon injury No.3 which are two parts A and B both are exit wounds. Since injury No.2 which was serious in nature and skin was dragged from the body due to injury that cannot specifically said that which exit wounds upon injury No.2 are. It is correct that injury No.1 was entry wound, injury no.3 was exit wound in two parts of injury No.1 and injury No.2 was observed on the hand. No other injury was observed upon the dead body during conducting post mortem. (emphasis supplied) Despite our repeated queries, both the learned Deputy Prosecutor General and the learned counsel for the complainant/respondent No.2 have failed to explain the said discrepancy in the prosecution evidence. In this manner, irreconcilable and distressing contradiction has cropped up in the ocular account of the occurrence as narrated by the prosecution witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) and the medical evidence as furnished by Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4). This contradiction in the ocular account of the occurrence, as narrated by Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) and the medical evidence as furnished by Dr. Asifa Zahid (PW-4), clearly establishes that the prosecution witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) miserably failed to prove their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence. Had the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW- 1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) seen the occurrence then there did not exist any possibility that they would have fallen into error, stating wrongly that the appellant fired at the deceased hitting her on her back. In this regard guidance is sought from the principle enunciated by the august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Amin Ali and another Vs. The State” (2011 SCMR 323) where the august Supreme Court of Pakistan was pleased to reject the evidence of injured witnesses and held as under:- “11.

In this regard guidance is sought from the principle enunciated by the august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Amin Ali and another Vs. The State” (2011 SCMR 323) where the august Supreme Court of Pakistan was pleased to reject the evidence of injured witnesses and held as under:- “11. All the three witnesses deposed that the deceased had received three injuries, but the Medical Officer found six injuries Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 12 on the person of the deceased. One of them had blackening. None of the witnesses deposed that any of the appellants had caused the injuries from a close range but on the contrary in the site plan the place of firing has been shown 8 feet away from the deceased. Thus from such a distance injury with blackening cannot be caused as it can be caused from a distance of less than 3 feet as per Modi's Medical Jurisprudence. The Medical Officer did not show as to which of the injury was entry or exit wound on the person of the deceased. The medical officer stated that metalic projectile was recovered from wound No.1/B which was an exit wound. If it was an exit wound then the metalic projectile would have been out of the body. The presence of metalic projectile in the body clearly establishes the fact that it is not an exit wound but an entry wound. The medical officer has not shown that any of the injuries had inverted or averted margins so as to ascertain as to which of the injuries is entry or exit wound. Thus on this count there is a conflict between the medical and oral evidence. Furthermore, according to Medical Officer, the P.W.15 had four injuries out of them two were entry and two were exit wounds but the P.Ws. 13 and 14 deposed that the injured had received three injuries. Thus the P.Ws. have shown one exit wound as entry wound. With regard to the injured Tanveer Hussain, the Medical Officer showed two injuries one entry wound on the chest and one exit wound on the back but all the three eye-witnesses deposed that P.W.14 had received two injuries on his chest.

Thus the P.Ws. have shown one exit wound as entry wound. With regard to the injured Tanveer Hussain, the Medical Officer showed two injuries one entry wound on the chest and one exit wound on the back but all the three eye-witnesses deposed that P.W.14 had received two injuries on his chest. As regards injuries on the person of Mst. Maqbool Bibi. The Medical Officer found one entry wound on her back with blackening, whereas P.Ws. 13, 14 and 15 deposed that the fire shot was fired from the roof of the shop. Entry wound with blackening marks cannot be caused from such a long distance. From the above position it is manifest that the ocular testimony is in conflict with the medical evidence. Thus, the deceased and injured did not receive the injuries in the manner, as alleged by the prosecution...................................... 13. From the above evidence of the P. Ws., they do not appear to be truthful witnesses; therefore, no implicit reliance can be placed on their evidence.” Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 13 Reliance is also placed on the case of “Mansab Ali Vs. The State” ( 2019 S C M R 1306 ) where the august Supreme Court of Pakistan was pleased to hold as under:-- “2. Prosecution case is structured upon ocular account. According to Muhammad Bilal, PW-6, the appellant made two successive shots on the deceased, first landing on the right arm, while second hitting the back; Muhammad Mansha, PW-7 took the same position. Both of them are in a diametrical conflict with the position taken in the crime report wherein the appellant is assigned solitary shot landing on the deceased's chest. Confusion is further compounded by the statement of Dr. Javid Ahmed, PW- 3, who noted an entry wound on the posterior aspect of left shoulder; the bullet exited from the chest valve; the second fire shot was on the right upper arm with its corresponding exit. This is not in line with the prosecution case that the appellant targeted the chest as the wound noted by the Medical Officer is an exit wound.

Javid Ahmed, PW- 3, who noted an entry wound on the posterior aspect of left shoulder; the bullet exited from the chest valve; the second fire shot was on the right upper arm with its corresponding exit. This is not in line with the prosecution case that the appellant targeted the chest as the wound noted by the Medical Officer is an exit wound. Both the witnesses have been duly confronted with their deviation from previous statements. From amongst the acquitted accused, Muhammad Yousaf was assigned a club blow to Muhammad Munawar, PW-8, noted by the Medical Officer as a painful swelling; his acquittal went without challenge. The learned Law Officer has not been able either to justify witnesses' departure from previous statements or to explain conflict between ocular account and medical evidence. Though with roles somewhat trivial nonetheless, en bloc acquittal of co-accused on the same evidence is yet another predicament facing the State. The prosecution case is fraught with doubts, thus it would be unsafe to maintain the conviction. Resultantly, the appeal is allowed, the impugned judgment is set aside. The appellant shall be set at liberty forthwith, if not required in any other case.” (emphasis supplied). 14. Another aspect of the case piquing our concern is the narrative of the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) that the occurrence took place at the brink of Kandair road (a rough path) when the witnesses along with the deceased were returning Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 14 from their land taken by them on lease, to which land they had proceeded in the morning at 10.00 a.m for the purpose of cutting crop and from which land they were returning after cutting the said crop. The said land where both the prosecution witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) had proceeded for the purpose of cutting crop along with the deceased was at a distance of as many as twelve acres.

Appeal No. 267 of 2017 14 from their land taken by them on lease, to which land they had proceeded in the morning at 10.00 a.m for the purpose of cutting crop and from which land they were returning after cutting the said crop. The said land where both the prosecution witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) had proceeded for the purpose of cutting crop along with the deceased was at a distance of as many as twelve acres. Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) during cross-examination stated as under:- “The inter se distance of place of occurrence towards place where we cut the crop of sugar cane is approximately 12 acre. On the day of occurrence at the time of occurrence, fifteen minutes consumed while arriving at the place of occurrence from the place where we harvesting sugar cane crop.” In this manner, it was admitted by the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) that the place of occurrence was not a place of their usual presence and was actually at a distance from the place where the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) had been present prior to the occurrence. In this manner, both the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) can validly be termed as chance witnesses and therefore were under a duty to prove their presence at the place of occurrence, at a time of occurrence by producing relevant and admissible evidence in this regard, however, both the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) failed to establish their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence. We have perused and analyzed the statements of both the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) and find that the details furnished by the said witnesses regarding their arrival Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 15 at the place of occurrence were inconsistent with each other. We have also noticed that the prosecution witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) failed to bring on record any physical proof of their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence.

Appeal No. 267 of 2017 15 at the place of occurrence were inconsistent with each other. We have also noticed that the prosecution witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) failed to bring on record any physical proof of their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence. According to Imam Bakhsh (PW-1), prior to the occurrence he along with the witnesses and the deceased had proceeded to the land taken by them on lease for purpose of cutting the sugar cane crop and for the said purpose he was carrying a sickle and a piece of cloth. Furthermore, Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) stated that each of them including him and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) after cutting the sugar cane crop, were carrying Gathris with them containing the cut crop when they arrived at the place of occurrence and witnessed the same during cross-examination Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) stated as under:- “Wgen (sic) we escorted towards sugar cane crop for harvesting at that time I was handing over sickle and cloth for wrapping the same after harvesting or cutting the sugar cane. We proceeded above all at our own land on the day of occurrence. The land upon which we proceeded on the day of occurrence is leased land which is being enjoyed by me.............................. On the day of occurrence, when we harvested the crop sugar cane, said sugar cane crop was cut by us in shape of three bundles and every person carried out each bundle after cutting the same. I do not know whether other persons were enjoying sickles for harvesting sugar cane crop however, myself were enjoying sickle for harvesting sugar cane crop. While proceedings, by way of carrying the said bundles of sugar cane crop which was carried respectively each person after harvesting sugar cane crop, Allah Rakhi was ahead of us empty handed and without carrying any bundle of sugar cane crop. I was chasing and Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Furthermore, it was the claim of the prosecution witnesses that in the midst of firing, the witness namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) did not receive even a single scratch during the whole occurrence. If the witness namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) had been present in the view of the assailants, then he would not Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 22 have been spared. Blessing Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) with such an incredible consideration and showing him such favour is implausible and opposed to the natural behaviour of any accused. It is all the more illogical that being perceptive of the fact that if the witnesses were left alive, they would depose against the accused, even then the appellant and his co-accused namely Akbar (since acquitted) did not cause any injury to them. Such behaviour, on part of the accused, runs counter to natural human conduct and behaviour. Article 129 of the Qanun-e-Shahadat Order, 1984 allows the courts to presume the existence of any fact, which it thinks likely to have happened, regard being had to the common course of natural events and human conduct in relation to the facts of the particular case. We, thus trust the existence of this fact, by virtue of the Article 129 of the Qanun-e- Shahadat Order, 1984, that the conduct of the assailants, as deposed to by the witnesses namely Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2), was opposed to the common course of natural events and human conduct. Hence, we are holding that both Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Ghulam Haider (PW-2) were not present at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence and did not witness the occurrence. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in its binding judgment has repeatedly held that in a scenario where the motivation was against the complainant or the witnesses but the accused did not cause any harm to them, notwithstanding being within the range of their firing, would reveal that the said witnesses were not present at the place of occurrence.

Allah Rakhi (deceased) on 29.01.2011 was duly proved, negating the claim of the prosecution witnesses and the demise of the motive alleged. Additionally allegedly the Nikah between Imam Bakhsh (PW-1) and Allah Rakhi (deceased) had taken place on 28.03.2011 whereas the occurrence took place on 24.01.2015 with no intervening episode of any dispute arising during this long period though the parties were residents of the same area. The prosecution witnesses failed to provide evidence enabling us to determine the truthfulness of the motive alleged, and the fact that the said motive was so compelling that it could have led the Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 32 appellant to have committed the Qatl-i-Amd of the deceased. There is a haunting silence with regard to the minutiae of motive alleged. No independent witness was produced by the prosecution to prove the motive as alleged. Even otherwise a tainted piece of evidence cannot corroborate another tainted piece of evidence. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan has held in the case of Muhammad Javed v. The State (2016 SCMR 2021) as under: “The said related and chance witnesses had failed to receive any independent corroboration inasmuch as no independent proof of the motive set up by the prosecution had been brought on the record of the case.” Furthermore, it is settled law that motive is a double-edge weapon, which can cut either way; if it is the reason for the appellant to murder the deceased, it equally is a ground for the complainant to falsely implicate him in this case. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan has held in the case of Muhammad Ashraf Alias Acchu v. The State (2019 S C M R 652) as under:- “7. The motive is always a double-edged weapon. The complainant Sultan Ahmad (PW9) has admitted murder enmity between the parties and has also given details of the same in his statement recorded before the trial court. No doubt, previous enmity can be a reason for the appellant to commit the alleged crime, but it can equally be a reason for the complainant side to falsely implicate the appellant in this case for previous grouse.” Reliance is also placed on the case of “Liaqat Ali and 11 others v.

Appeal No. 267 of 2017 33 case of Allah Bakhsh and another v. The State (PLD 1978 SC 171).” It is an admitted rule of appreciation of evidence that motive is only corroborative pieces of evidence and if the ocular account is found to be unreliable then motive and recovery have no evidentiary value and lost their significance. 20. Considering all the above circumstances, we entertain serious doubt in our minds regarding the involvement of the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim in the present case. It is a settled principle of law that for giving the benefit of the doubt it is not necessary that there should be so many circumstances rather if only a single circumstance creating reasonable doubt in the mind of a prudent person is available then such benefit is to be extended to an accused not as a matter of concession but as of right. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Muhammad Mansha Vs. The State” (2018 SCMR 772) has enunciated the following principle: “Needless to mention that while giving the benefit of doubt to an accused it is not necessary that there should be many circumstances creating doubt. If there is a circumstance which creates reasonable doubt in a prudent mind about the guilt of the accused, then the accused would be entitled to the benefit of such doubt, not as a matter of grace and concession, but as a matter of right.

The State” (2018 SCMR 772) has enunciated the following principle: “Needless to mention that while giving the benefit of doubt to an accused it is not necessary that there should be many circumstances creating doubt. If there is a circumstance which creates reasonable doubt in a prudent mind about the guilt of the accused, then the accused would be entitled to the benefit of such doubt, not as a matter of grace and concession, but as a matter of right. It is based on the maxim, "it is better that ten guilty persons be acquitted rather than one innocent person be convicted". Reliance in this behalf can be made upon the cases of Tariq Pervez v. The State (1995 SCMR 1345), Ghulam Qadir and 2 others v. The State (2008 SCMR 1221), Muhammad Akramv.The State (2009 SCMR 230) and Muhammad Zamanv.The State (2014 SCMR 749).” Reliance is also placed on the judgment of the august Supreme Court of Pakistan Muhammad Akram Vs. the State (2009 SCMR 230) in which it has been observed in paragraph No.13 of page 236 as infra: Murder Reference No. 22 of 2017 Crl. Appeal No. 267 of 2017 34 “It is an axiomatic principle of law that in case of doubt, the benefit thereof must accrue in favour of the accused as matter of right and not of grace. It was observed by this Court in the case of Tariq Pervez v. The State 1995 SCMR 1345 that for giving the benefit of doubt, it was not necessary that there should be many circumstances creating doubts. If there is circumstance which created reasonable doubt in a prudent mind about the guilt of the accused, then the accused would be entitled to the benefit of doubt not as a matter of grace and concession but as a matter of right.” 21. For what has been discussed above, the Criminal Appeal No.267 of 2017 lodged by Azhar son of Hashim (appellant) is allowed and the conviction and sentence of the appellant namely Azhar son of Hashim awarded by the learned trial court through the impugned judgment dated 28.04.2017 are hereby set-aside. Azhar son of Hashim (appellant) is ordered to be acquitted by extending him the benefit of doubt. Azhar son of Hashim (appellant) is in custody and he is directed to be released forthwith if not required in any other case. 22.
